components.text: Electro-Sun nightly audit complete. {{1}} proposals waiting for review. Lessons: {{2}}, Facts: {{3}}, Code notes: {{4}}. Open the admin Nightly Audit tab to review and approve.

_notes.purpose: Permanent-delivery version of the nightly self-improvement audit ping (src/audit.js > sendOwnerAuditPing). The free-form ping rides the 24h customer-service window, so a ping sent at 21:00 Africa/Lagos to a recipient who has been silent for more than 24h is accepted by Meta (whatsapp.send.ok logs) but silently dropped (confirmed 2026-06-16: run #1, 164 findings, never delivered to AUDIT_PING_WHATSAPP). An approved template is not window-bound.
_notes.category_reason: UTILITY: an operational notification about a system process, not marketing. Meta is generally lenient on internal-facing UTILITY templates (see owner_hourly_report_en).
_notes.recipient: Sent to AUDIT_PING_WHATSAPP (the developer line, currently 966502392650), NOT the owner. The owner is deliberately kept out of audit/developer pings during the soak.
_notes.button_note: Static URL button to /admin (no per-run deep link). A dynamic suffix button with #audit={{run_id}} was considered but a fragment in a dynamic URL button is an approval risk (see owner_escalation_alert_en open questions); the admin Nightly Audit tab auto-selects the most recent run, so the static link is sufficient. The free-form fallback (buildOwnerAuditPing) still carries the exact #audit=<id> deep link when the window is open.
_notes.code_wiring: src/audit.js > sendOwnerAuditPing tries this template first and falls back to the free-form sendMessage if the template send fails (e.g. while still PENDING approval), so the deploy is safe regardless of approval state.
_notes.how_to_submit: META_WABA_ID must be the live WABA (986225450549617) in .env. Already added to TEMPLATE_FILES in scripts/submit_templates.js. Run: node scripts/submit_templates.js. Check status with: node scripts/check_templates.js.
